name: McCutcheon v. Federal Election Commission
summary: Struck down aggregate limits on the total amount individuals could contribute to all candidates, parties, and PACs combined, finding they did not serve an anti-corruption interest given other existing safeguards.
significance: Both sides heavily relied on this case. Petitioners argue it rejected the conduit-bribery theory underlying Colorado II. Defenders note McCutcheon specifically pointed to coordinated expenditure limits as a safeguard justifying its ruling.
